title: astilbe japonica subsp. glaberrima
intro: Astilbe japonica subsp. glaberrima is a plant species in the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae) that is native to Japan and is a subspecies of the Astilbe japonica. This plant is also known as Japanese astilbe or Japanese false goat's beard and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots of the plant are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
